name: GameCI
description: Free CI/CD tooling for Unity projects on GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, and CircleCI. Provides Docker images and workflow templates for automated build, test, and deploy pipelines.

seo.title: GameCI — Game Development Tools
seo.meta_description: GameCI: Free CI/CD tooling for Unity projects on GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, and CircleCI. Provides Docker images and workflow templates for automated build, tes

when_to_use: Automated Unity build pipeline triggered on every GitHub push
Nightly test run catching regression in Unity project on CI
Multi-platform Unity build (Windows, Mac, WebGL) in parallel jobs
Automated deployment of Unity WebGL build to itch.io
Pull request preview build linked in GitHub PR comments

pros: Free, open-source CI templates for Unity projects
Docker images cover most Unity versions and targets
Cuts CI setup from days to hours for small teams
Works across GitHub Actions, GitLab, and CircleCI
cons: Unity-only, nothing for Unreal or Godot projects
Build times on free GitHub runners are painful
License activation on CI can be fragile
Maintained by volunteers, response times vary
review.verdict: Essential shortcut if youre automating Unity builds; accept that you own the self-hosted runner headaches.
review.best_for: Indie Unity teams automating builds and tests on GitHub Actions or GitLab
review.not_for: Unreal, Godot, or enterprise Unity shops needing vendor-backed CI support
